text: Aanu-mi ka nnye libiɔ lɛmɛni kɔtu, na si ulekubi ɔdi úbo ɔnii ubedu ntu ni, ndubɔsu-nwu nnii, “Buvele du ntu blii bɔdi na ƒula libui kemmo te-mi na lɛni”
translated: When you see a palm tree bending down, and there is a rope tied to its top, don't ask, "Why is this tree bending like it wants to fall to the ground for me to climb?"

ref: After Isaac had been there a long time, King Abimelech looked out a window and saw Isaac hugging and kissing Rebekah.
text: Ka Isak úlie mmɔ útu uwi geen kama ni, Abimelek unye nwuna ƒansili na ɔ́nu Isak úmene ka ɔda ulekudi ɔɔƒuƒwɛ na ɔɔƒianku-nwu.
translated: When Isaac had been there a long time, Abimelech king of the Philistines looked down from a window and saw Isaac caressing his wife Rebekah.

ref: Go and kill two of your best young goats and bring them to me. I'll cook the tasty food that your father loves so much.
text: Sie bula bebuo akɔ na atádu bese bakɔdi inyɔ eboku-mi. Na ndubuti-ma lɛnwa adila abuni ani udu na ƒula ɔti ɔɔyɔ́ lite-nwu kalɛ geen na ɔwia-nya.
translated: They told the children not to go near the river bank. And the young ones listened to the advice because they knew that the water was very deep and dangerous.

ref: Jacob went to his father and said, “Father, here I am.” “Which one of my sons are you?” his father asked.
text: Amu na úti adila ammɔ úsienku na ɔ́kpali ɔnini, “Ati.” Mmɔ ni, ɔti lɛnɔ ɔ́tuna ɔnini, “Mina ubi, awɔ ɔma na ɔdi?”
translated: When they asked the child but the child answered them, "Father." Then, they asked him again, "Your father, where does he stay?"

ref: So Isaac told him, “Serve me the wild meat, and I can give you my blessing.” Jacob gave him some meat, and he ate it. He also gave him some wine, and he drank it.
text: Mmɔ kani Isak ɔ́su-nwu ɔnini, “Mina ubi, ti lɛla lɛni na átádu mmɔ boku-mi na lɛkun na lite-ɔ mina kusɛ kusɛ.” Amu na Yakob úti ala ammɔ úboku-nwu na ɔ́di. Na úti ntɛ mini mini bɔdi lɛnɔ úboku-nwu na ɔ́ni.
translated: When Isaac was old, he said to his son, "My son, bring me some game to eat so that I may bless you before I die." But Jacob took the food to him and he ate. And he took wine also and he drank it.

ref: Isaac answered, “Your brother tricked me and stole your blessing.”
text: Nsu ɔti ɔ́su-nwu ɔnini, “Ƒ'ɔnwani úbo úbatɔlɔ-mi na ɔ́ƒɔ ƒula kusɛ kusɛ.”
translated: "The water said to the fire, "Your friend will help you when you are in great trouble.""

ref: Esau found out that his father Isaac had blessed Jacob and had warned him not to marry any of the Canaanite women. He also learned that Jacob had been sent to find a wife in northern Syria
text: Ka Yakob ɔ́tɔlɔ ni, Esau úbenu ka ɔti Isak úte Yakob kusɛ kusɛ na údie-nwu ulu na ɔ́tɔlɔ úsie Mesopotamia kalɔ lɛlɔ ɔnii utéti uleku na mmɔ. Na uwi ɔni na úte-nwu kusɛ kusɛ ni, úbo-nwu ɔda ɔnini, “Teti Kanaanni uleku ɔdi.”
translated: When Jacob left, Esau saw that Isaac blessed Jacob greatly and sent him to Mesopotamia to find a wife there. And he saw that when he blessed him greatly, he commanded him saying, "Do not marry a Canaanite woman."

ref: When Jacob got up early the next morning, he took the rock that he had used for a pillow and stood it up as a place of worship. Then he poured olive oil on the rock to dedicate it to God,
text: Ka kadi kégye ɔtanta ni, Yakob ɔ́ta na úti lɛtabi lɛni lɛlɔ na ɔ́sia lili mmɔ úku únyene na ɔ́bla akpala na mmɔ na úti nnɔ úwi-ni lili.
translated: And when morning came, Jacob took the stone that he had put under his head and set it up as a pillar and poured oil on top of it.

ref: and he named the place Bethel. Before that it had been named Luz.
text: Na ɔ́sia mmɔ lɛnyi ɔnii Betel, na nka ɔma ɔmmɔ na bátaalɔ banii Lus.
translated: And he called the name of that place Bethel, but the name of the city was formerly Luz.

ref: He then kissed Rachel and started crying because he was so happy.
text: Ka ɔ́wɔlɔ ni, ɔ́kya Rahel kɔgbu na úse kalɔ uuwulo momoomo.
translated: When she died, they buried Rahel on the way to Ephrath which is Bethlehem.

ref: As soon as Laban heard the news, he ran out to meet Jacob. He hugged and kissed him and brought him to his home, where Jacob told him everything that had happened.
text: Ka Laban únu Yakob kɔku kani na úkeli útásiaku-nwu aani ɔnwani ubidi kalɛ. Útákya-nwu kɔgbu na ɔ́kpanku-nwu úsienku nwuna kaduli. Mmɔ na Yakob ɔ́sɔku lɛla biala ɔ́su-nwu kaduɛ.
translated: Then Laban kissed Jacob's children and his daughters and blessed them. He got up and returned to his home. And Jacob also went on his journey.

ref: The Lord knew that Jacob loved Rachel more than he did Leah, and so he gave children to Leah, but not to Rachel.
text: Ka Unwoti Katɔti ɔ́nu ka Yakob ulayɔ Lea kaduɛ ɔsu ni, ɔ́lɛmi-nwu kaƒɔ mmomu na anwu mɔɔwɔ. Na Rahel anwu uláwɔ.
translated: When God saw that Jacob loved Lea's co-wife more than her, he opened her womb so that she could bear children. But Rachel's womb was closed.

ref: Rachel said, “I've struggled hard with my sister, and I've won!” So she named the boy Naphtali.
text: Amu na Rahel ɔ́su ɔnini, “Kekpo geen na líkpo ku mina ɔnwani ulekubi na lɛ́di lɛlɔ.” Anwuɔsu ɔ́sia ubi ɔmmɔ lɛnyi ɔnii Naƒtali, na nwuna kalɔ ndi kekpo.
translated: When Rachel bore a son, she said, "God has judged me and has also heard my voice and given me a son." Therefore she called his name Naphtali, which means 'my struggle'.

ref: That evening when Jacob came in from the fields, Leah told him, “You're sleeping with me tonight. I hired you with my son's love flowers.” They slept together that night,
text: Anwuɔsu uwi ɔni na Yakob ɔ́si kemo úbeduo useyi ɔmmɔ ni, Lea útásiaku-nwu ulu na ɔ́su-nwu ɔnini, “Ɔdi ka abatɛ-mi kudɔ ɔmwi. Ɔsika lɛ́ƒɔ-ɔ líse ɔkpa na kɔwɔ ligyiitu lɛni na Rahel ɔ́ƒɔ mina ubi kudɔ ɔsu.” Anwuɔsu úsie útátɛ Lea kudɔ usien ɔmmɔ.
translated: When God saw that Jacob loved Rachel more than Leah, he opened Leah's womb so that she could bear children, but he closed Rachel's womb so that she could not bear children. God did this to comfort Leah because of her suffering.

ref: Later, Leah had a daughter and named her Dinah.
text: Na uwi ɔdi kama ni, Lea úbawɔ ulekubi úte Yakob na ɔ́sia-nwu lɛnyi ɔnii Dina.
translated: After he had done this, Leah bore a daughter to Jacob and she named her Dinah.

ref: Before the end of the day, Laban had separated his spotted and speckled animals and the black lambs from the others and had put his sons in charge of them.
text: Anwuɔsu údie bebuo bagyani ku beledi bani naaadi baƒualadi sɔɔn úte nwuna bebi bananabi ka bataanu-ma lɛlɔ.
translated: In the past, some people used to go and fetch water from the river early in the morning before others woke up.

ref: and he told them: Your father isn't as friendly with me as he used to be, but the God my ancestors worshiped has been on my side.
text: Ka bébo ni, ɔ́su-ma ɔnini, “Lɛ́nu lɛ́ti ka bina ɔti ɔbladu na mina lɛlɔ ɔ́vumana. Na ɔsɔ uuuse aani kudeedi kalɛ. Nsu na ɔmmɔ ƒui kama ni, mina Katɔti ɔni ndi mina ɔti Unwoti Katɔti uliku-mi.
translated: When he arrived, he told them, "The time has come for the Son of Man to be glorified. I tell you the truth, unless a grain of wheat falls to the ground and dies, it remains alone. But if it dies, it produces many seeds."

ref: He treats us like foreigners and has even cheated us out of the bride price that should have been ours.
text: Ɔƒɛni ni, baƒɔ kani na ɔɔnu-bu. Use aani buye kani na úti-bu úye. Ɔsika kavumi kani na báƒɔ na bula lili ni, ɔ́ɔdi egulabi ammɔ ƒui.
translated: Today, they will go to the farm. The children will also go to their houses. The rich man will go with them to the town, he bought a car yesterday.

ref: Three days later Laban found out that Jacob had gone.

translated: When Jacob heard these words, Laban came and spoke with Jacob.

ref: he told his men to get some more rocks and pile them up next to it. Then Jacob and Laban ate a meal together beside the rocks.
text: Na ɔ́su nwuna lɛlɔ bekyuli ɔnini, “Bɛla atabi bɛsala.” Na ama lɛnɔ bála atabi adi básala lisu. Na bélie mmɔ bádi na atabi lisu lɛmmɔ kudɔ.
translated: And the chief told his children, saying, "They should bring chairs." And the people brought chairs and they sat down. And they ate food together and chairs down there in the room.

ref: Then Laban said: If you mistreat my daughters or marry other women, I may not know about it, but remember, God is watching us!
text: Si átada mina bebi belekubi kaduɛ iye ásɔ ététi beleku bamba na bana kama ni, Unwoti Katɔti ɔɔnu. Ina ka ukyuli bamba ɔdi uuubunku-bu na mɛni ni, Unwoti Katɔti ndi ami ku-ɔ ƒui kenye udiene.”
translated: If you want to drink my blood and eat my flesh and become one with me, listen to the True Word. If you follow the word and do good deeds, the True Word will give you eternal life.

ref: Jacob killed an animal and offered it as a sacrifice there on the mountain, and he invited his men to eat with him. After the meal they spent the night on the mountain.
text: Na ɔ́bla akpala na kalɔ kammɔ lɛlɔ na abi lɛlɔ mmɔ. Na ɔ́lɔ anwu badi ƒui ka bebo babadi. Na ka bádi báwɔlɔ ni, bátɛ mmɔ.
translated: And he took a basket and put food in it and put water in it. And he went to the farm to give it to the children who were working. And when they had finished working, they came home.

ref: And Jacob told them to be sure to say that he was right behind them. Jacob hoped the gifts would make Esau friendly, so Esau would be glad to see him when they met.
text: Na bitayiƒala ka bɛsu-nwu bɛnini, ‘Ƒula kabla ɔblane Yakob ɔkpɛ kama ɔdubo.’ ” Yakob ɔ́su-ma lɛni ɔsika ɔ́bla kasusu ɔnini, “Ndubuti etela ani na líti lɛ́tualaku lɛtɔtɔ mɛni lɛyala-nwu kaƒɔ. Na ɔmmɔ kama si uwi ɔni na ami ku-nwu búbasa anumi ku anumi ni, ɔƒɛni kalɛ ɔdubo enu bugyingya na ɔƒɔ-mi.”
translated: And the midwives said to Pharaoh, 'Before the midwife arrives to Jacob, they have already given birth.' " Jacob answered him saying, "The work that you have given me to do is very difficult. If he comes to kill me and my children and my wives, then he will take everything and leave me."

translated: When he died, Israel gathered all his twelve sons together and blessed each one of them. The blessings he gave were according to Jacob's words.

ref: Then Jacob prayed: You, Lord, are the God who was worshiped by my grandfather Abraham and by my father Isaac. You told me to return home to my family, and you promised to be with me and make me successful.
text: Ɔmmɔ kama ni, Yakob ɔ́kpali katɔ na úvele ɔnini, “O mina Unwoti, Abraham Katɔti ku Isak Katɔti, awɔ ɔni násu-mi anini, ‘Nwini na eteesie ƒula kalɔ lɛlɔ ku ƒula lɛlɔ bekyuli kudɔ na ndubɔya na enu ku akɔkɔ eyi-ɔ.’
translated: That night, Jacob got up and prayed to God, saying, "O God of my father Abraham and God of my father Isaac, you who said to me, 'Return to your country and to your relatives, and I will make you prosper.'"

ref: And Jacob told them to be sure to say that he was right behind them. Jacob hoped the gifts would make Esau friendly, so Esau would be glad to see him when they met.
text: Na bitayiƒala ka bɛsu-nwu bɛnini, ‘Ƒula kabla ɔblane Yakob ɔkpɛ kama ɔdubo.’ ” Yakob ɔ́su-ma lɛni ɔsika ɔ́bla kasusu ɔnini, “Ndubuti etela ani na líti lɛ́tualaku lɛtɔtɔ mɛni lɛyala-nwu kaƒɔ. Na ɔmmɔ kama si uwi ɔni na ami ku-nwu búbasa anumi ku anumi ni, ɔƒɛni kalɛ ɔdubo enu bugyingya na ɔƒɔ-mi.”

And if they come with their brothers against me and my children, then I will surely die and they will kill me."

ref: Jacob said, “I have seen God face to face, and I am still alive.” So he named the place Peniel.
text: Ɔmmɔ ɔsu ni, Yakob ɔ́sia mmɔ lɛnyi ɔnii Peniel, na ɔ́su ɔnini, “Lɛ́sia mɛni lɛnyi lɛni, ɔsika lɛ́nu Unwoti Katɔti anumi ku anumi na útayila-mi ngba.”
translated: When the sun rose, Jacob passed through that place called Peniel, and he was limping because God had touched his hip joint and it was injured.

ref: and now I own cattle, donkeys, and sheep, as well as many slaves. Master, I am sending these messengers in the hope that you will be kind to me.”
text: Na mbo ɔmantɛ bakɔ ku bebuo ala betine ku baƒonu ku bese ku kabla bablane bananabi ku belekubi. Na ɔƒaani ni, mina Ati, mɔɔsia-a kenye kamɛni kalɛ na mina lɛlɔ lɛda-a ebiti.’ ”
translated: And the chief gathered all the elders and the young men and the women and the children, about twelve people and the sick ones. And he said to them, 'I, Father, have shown you a good thing that I want you to know about.'

ref: But you have promised that I would be a success and that someday it will be as hard to count my descendants as it is to count the grains of sand along the seashore.
text: Nsu awɔ násu-mi anini, ‘Ndubɔnu ka enu ku akɔkɔ éyi-ɔ. Na ndubɔya na ƒula bebi ku bawɔ bemomo aani ɛpu kɔnwinimi. Na ukyuli ɔdi ulakana-ma bɔka.’ ”
translated: The water said to them, "The world is not like before. And people now do bad things and they do not fear God. And the sky has become dark."

ref: When the man saw that he could not win, he struck Jacob on the hip and threw it out of joint.
text: Ka ɔnana ɔmmɔ ɔɔɔdaku tɔ Yakob lɛlɔ bɔkana bɔdi ni, ɔ́wɔ Yakob kasɔma likubi na líkpeli.
translated: When the old man heard what Jacob his grandson had done, he called Jacob and blessed him.

ref: You can share this land with us. Move freely about until you find the property you want; then buy it and settle down here.
text: Bɛkana na bilie-bu akɔ, ɔsika kalɔ kewi kete-mii ƒaa. Bilie na bɛdi kuye na bɛbla akɛla pii bite lɛlɔ.”
translated: When they arrived at the village, the chief gathered all the people. He told them the story and they all rejoiced greatly.

ref: Shechem added, “Do this favor for me, and I'll give whatever you want.
text: Ɔmmɔ kama ni, Sekem ɔ́su Dina ɔti ku banwani ɔnini, “Bɛya na mina lɛlɔ lɛda-mii ebiti, na lɛla biala lɛni na bɛdubuye-mi ni, ndubɔbla lite-mii.
translated: And when Shechem spoke to Dinah's father and brothers saying, "Let me find favor in your eyes, and whatever you say to me I will give, ask me for as great a bride price and gift as you will."

ref: So they tricked them by saying: You're not circumcised! It would be a disgrace for us to let you marry Dinah now.
text: Na básu-ma banini, “Bulakana lɛla lɛmmɔ udu bɔbla. Bulakana na buti bula ɔnwani bute kadi kani ukyuli nétekulo lego, ɔsika ɔdi-bu kɔƒa.
translated: They told the children, "The Lord has given us this land. The Lord wants us to go and take it, but there are strong people living there, so it will be difficult."

ref: So everyone gave Jacob their idols and their earrings, and he buried them under the oak tree near Shechem.
text: Ka ɔ́su-ma lɛni ni, bálɛku baƒiɔ lidu ƒui béboku-nwu na Yakob úku-ma ɔ́kya kugyii bonwu kɔdi nnye mmɔ kalɔ na Sekem mmɔ.
translated: And after that, the children of Israel took the women and children of Jacob's house who were left in the land of Canaan and went to Shechem.

ref: I will give you the land that I promised Abraham and Isaac, and it will belong to your family forever.
text: Na kalɔ kani na líti líte Abraham ku Isak ni, aka kani na líti muute-ɔ. Na kalɔ kamɛni na ndubuti lite ƒula kaba kemmo bebi.”
translated: The promise that was made to Abraham and Isaac, that promise was fulfilled. The promise that was spoken has now come to pass."

ref: the third was Basemath, who was Ishmael's daughter and Nebaioth's sister.
text: Na uleku ɔtɛdi ndi Basmat. Na ɔti ndi Ismael na ɔnwani ulekubi ndi Nebaiot.
translated: And the name of the third was Basemath. And she was the daughter of Ishmael and the sister of Nebaioth.

ref: All of these clans descended from Esau, who was known as Edom.
text: Bekyuli bani anyi na búnu mmɔ ndi baga bani nálɛ bási Esau, ɔni baalɔ lɛnɔ banii Edom, kaba bebi akɔ.
translated: These are the generations of Esau, who is Edom, in the hill country of Seir.

ref: Dishan's sons were Uz and Aran.
text: Disan bebi bananabi ndi Us ku Aran.
translated: The child saw the children in the house at Aran.

ref: Jacob lived in the land of Canaan, where his father Isaac had lived,
text: Kanaan kalɔ kani kemmo na Yakob ɔti ɔ́kya ni, mmɔ kani na Yakob lɛnɔ úlie.
translated: Canaan was the place where Jacob's father lived, and it was where Jacob also stayed.

ref: which showed that Joseph was his favorite son, and so Joseph's brothers hated him and would not be friendly to him.
text: Ka nwuna banwani bánu báti ka bana ɔti ɔɔyɔ́ Yoseƒ teetulo-ma ɔsu ni, balátɛmiku-nwu ku lɛdi buɛɛ munu munu. Na beekisi-nwu.
translated: When the man's sons saw that their father loved Joseph more than them, they hated him and could not speak peacefully to him. They despised him.

ref: “Don't murder him or even harm him. Just throw him into a well out here in the desert.” Reuben planned to rescue Joseph later and take him back to his father.
text: Na bitaya na buwi ubugya kalɔ. Bibo-nwu bɛkya abiɔ amɛni ɔdi kemmo na ketele mɛni. Nsu bitetuku-nwu ulu bamba ɔdi lɛlɔ munu munu.” Ruben kasusu ndi ka ɔɔsu-ma lɛni na kama na udie-nwu ɔsɔku usienku bana ɔti.
translated: They went and found the child there. His brothers wanted to kill him but they did not kill him. They threw him into a deep pit that was very very deep." Reuben thought that he would come back and take the child out of the pit to return him.

ref: Then he went back to his brothers and said, “The boy is gone! What am I going to do?”
text: Na úsie nwuna banwani kudɔ útasu-ma ɔnini, “Ɔnamu ɔmmɔ ɔsɔ uuubo mmɔ! Dɛ mabla?”
translated: "When the child saw his friends coming towards him, he said, "You people have come well! How are you?"

ref: So Joseph lived in the home of Potiphar, his Egyptian owner. Soon Potiphar realized that the Lord was helping Joseph to be successful in whatever he did.
text: Ka nwuna ɔtɔlati ɔ́nu ɔ́ti kalɛ na Unwoti Katɔti uliku Yoseƒ ku kalɛ na nwuna ablala aatɛ-nwu ɔsu ni,
translated: When the child was born, he gave him the name that the Angel of the Lord had told Joseph to give to the child,

ref: Potiphar's wife grabbed hold of his coat and said, “Make love to me!” Joseph ran out of the house, leaving his coat there in her hands.
text: Amu na uleku ɔmɛni útéduo-nwu kudɔ mmɔ na úbo ɔ́da Yoseƒ na ɔ́su-nwu ɔnini, “Bo na aya na bɔtɛ!” Mmɔ na Yoseƒ ɔ́nwanɛ lɛlɔ ɔ́lɛ, na nwuna awu gyɔlɔɔ ɔmmɔ ɔkya uleku ɔmmɔ enu kemmo.
translated: And when the chief baker saw that the interpretation was good, he said to Joseph, "I also in my dream!" And Joseph answered him saying, that within three days Pharaoh would cut off his head from his body.

ref: While Joseph was in prison, both the king's personal servant and his chief cook made the king angry. So he had them thrown into the same prison with Joseph.
text: Ɔmmɔ ɔ́su ɔ́ya na bákya-ma ɔtɔ na bagyumanane bammɔ ƒui unengu kesii. Na mmɔ kani na bákya Yoseƒ lɛnɔ ɔtɛ.
translated: They took the body down from the cross and wrapped it in a new linen cloth. And they laid Joseph in it.

ref: Three days later, while the king was celebrating his birthday with a dinner for his officials, he sent for his personal servant and the chief cook.
text: Ɔƒɛni limeyi lɛtɛdi lɛmmɔ ni, lɛdi Ƒarao lɛwɔmeyi. Anwuɔsu ɔ́ya na bánwa adila na ɔ́lɔ nwuna benengu ƒui ka babadi limeyi bete-nwu. Na kasakɔ mmɔ ni, Ƒarao ɔ́ya na bákpanku ntɛ ku bodobodo lɛlɔ banune bammɔ bébenyene bana benengu bakladi bammɔ ƒui anumi.
translated: When these things happened, they troubled Pharaoh. The next day he called all the wise men and all the magicians to come and interpret the dreams for him. But when this happened, Pharaoh saw that none of the people who came could tell him what the dreams meant.

ref: ate the fat, healthy cows. When this happened, the king woke up.
text: Bebuo bani nákwii mɛni béƒie bani nélie lɛmwi mmɔ bébweli. Na ka ɔ́nu lɛla lɛmɛni ni, Ƒarao úkeyi lɛlɔ ɔ́gyinya.
translated: The people did not listen to Moses because of their broken spirit and harsh slavery. And when he spoke to Pharaoh, Pharaoh hardened his heart.

ref: The thin heads of grain swallowed the seven full heads. Again the king woke up, and it had only been a dream.
text: Na ebi ƒaƒaa amɛni ébweli ebi abuni. Mmɔ ni, Ƒarao ɔ́sɔ ɔ́gyinya, na ɔ́bla-nwu nwaa ka kɔna kɔdi na úgye.
translated: And it happened in the morning that his spirit was troubled. So Pharaoh sent and called all the magicians and wise men of Egypt.

ref: One night we both had dreams, and each dream had a different meaning.
text: Mmɔ ni, bula inyɔ ƒui búgye ana adi usien unwi kemmo, na kɔna biala ku lɛla lɛni na kɔdanku.
translated: Today, when you see someone doing something bad, you should advise them so that they can change their ways.
